data.description:
## Why
`_reclaim_if_stale_index_lock` reads the lock's mtime, judges it stale, then later
`os.remove`s the same pathname **without re-confirming it is the same file**
(`src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py:88-107`; mtime read at ~:100, unconditional remove at
~:103-105). A peer can remove the stale lock and create a fresh **live** `index.lock`
between the stat and the unlink, and rebar then unlinks the peer's new lock. VALIDATED:
no test replaces the inode during reclamation (existing tests cover only a stationary
stale lock and a young lock released later).

## What
Add a production hardening that re-validates identity+age immediately before removal
(compare `st_dev`/`st_ino` and mtime from a single `os.stat`/`os.open` handle, or an
equivalent safe-revalidation), and a deterministic regression test.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py` — `_reclaim_if_stale_index_lock`.
- Test: pause reclamation after the stale file is statted (injected seam, not sleep),
  replace it with a fresh file, resume; assert the replacement survives.
- Do not change the stale threshold constant or the retry loop's public behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A deterministic test reproduces inode-replacement-during-reclaim and asserts the
      freshly-created lock is NOT unlinked.
- [ ] The reclaim path re-checks file identity (device+inode) and age at the moment of
      removal; a mismatch aborts the unlink.
- [ ] Existing stationary-stale and young-lock ride-out tests still pass.
- [ ] `make test` green.
data.title: Harden index.lock stale-reclaim TOCTOU + deterministic replacement test
